module_mood_contract: Joyful creation — 'it works, on my repo.' Student discovers the compound loop on a real bug from their own backlog; the artifact (CLAUDE.local.md born from session evidence) is unambiguously theirs. Any edit that makes M1 feel like a TDD tutorial or a tools-setup module steals the personal-authorship feeling that sets up M2's grounded-competence deepening.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands at 8+ per phase and close.
Mood doesn't resolve the next module's teaching moment early.
Named teaching moment reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The compound step (Ex3: CLAUDE.local.md born from session evidence) is forced inside the exercise's own structure. The Debrief block narrates this; the exercise file carries the prompt. A student cannot complete Ex3 without running the retro — the artifact is the evidence. Push-back moves for compound rubber-stamping are enumerated in the maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea honors the strategy doc's M1 mood contract and framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea ('The first compound loop closes on a trivial bug from your own backlog. Orient, fix tests-first without plan mode, retro into a personal CLAUDE.local.md born from the session, close the bug's ticket via one connector.') matches strategy doc M1 detail precisely — three exercises, same sequence, same artifacts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: This file's close hands off to M2's open hook.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 43: 'Module 2 is where plan mode earns its keep: multi-file work, a second pass that pressure-tests the plan...' — matches M2's grounded-competence mood and plan-mode-at-depth arc. CLAUDE.local.md at the top of the next session is explicitly named as the M2 context-carry artifact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:19 — 'The room harvests everyone's before the training adds its own.' This is a cohort-management sentence in student-facing body prose. Rule 27 bans authored cohort-management beats. Diagnostic: the sentence is about COHORT MANAGEMENT (room harvesting move), not the WORK. Not a full authored conversation, but it's a room-action embedded in body text. Unchanged from previous cycle.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cut the sentence; the question stands without it. The trainer runs the harvest without a body cue.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module file contains no fenced prompt blocks — forcing functions live in the referenced exercise files (orient-and-introspect.md, fix-tests-first.md, compound-and-close.md). The module file's student-facing body describes the arc; it doesn't embed prose forcing functions. Architectural split is clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 89-93 now contain an explicit '## Leap test' section (per check_pedagogy.md rule 45) with three named falsifiable outcomes: (1) runs /context on a working session without being prompted — falsifiable by scrollback; (2) writes a failing test that proves the bug exists before touching the fix — falsifiable by commit history or diff; (3) edits ./CLAUDE.local.md from session evidence after a non-trivial session with a rule that quotes the specific session beat — falsifiable by rule naming a session moment. All three use named verbs and named artifacts; none are competency claims. Previous REVISE resolved.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function's dominant failure mode and one recovery move documented.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block lines 102-114 now contain an explicit '## Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch' table (per check_pedagogy.md rule 47) covering all forcing functions: Connections trick, Ex1 introspection prompt, Ex1 /context command, Ex2 tests-first, Ex2 diff review, Ex3 compound-writes-CLAUDE.local.md, Ex3 team-worthy flag, Ex3 quoted-evidence, Ex3 MCP install. Each row names the dominant failure mode and a single escape hatch (trainer/Nerd push or fallback path). Format matches rule 47's diagnostic requirements. Carries forward from previous PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — produced files listed as contract rows with stable path + producing prompt + consuming module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Rule 46 specifies 'Technical judge blocks ship on missing contract rows.' This is a technical-class finding. Story class notes that lines 94-100 now contain an '## Artefact contracts' section with a contract table for ./CLAUDE.local.md (stable identifier, producing prompt, consuming modules). Presence is confirmed; correctness and completeness of contract rows deferred to the technical judge.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Plug points are integration commitments, not labels; UX — operator can paste each prompt without inventing setup.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Plug Points in maintainer block name concrete org-context kinds: 'Student's repo (chosen in prework) + bug Claude surfaced in prework + sponsor-stated ticket tracker.' These are integration commitments (exercise prompts downstream depend on repo + bug + tracker being named). UX: module has no fenced prompts of its own; all prompts live in exercise files. A fast operator skipping body reads: Big Idea → exercises linked by name → Debrief summary → Bridge. No 'wait, what?' moments at module level. Exercise-level UX out of scope for this judge.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers; demo lectures (ceiling-showing) are openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 21: '[The wizard move]' — opener position. Strategy and module both describe this as a ceiling-showing demo (trainer demos a wizard-level move on a volunteer's codebase). Correct opener placement per rule 1. Line 40: '[How this training was built]' — placed after Debrief in closer position. This is a meta-frame lecture (naming a pattern the student just lived through). Correct closer placement.

M1 is not a contrast-mood module (it is joyful creation, not a contrast arc like M5). Rule 2 targets M5-style contrast-mood modules. One opener lecture (wizard move) before exercises is minimal. N/A for contrast-mood constraint; PASS for dosage check.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honored — does not flatten or pre-resolve.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block: 'Mood target: joyful creation — it works, on my repo.' Explicit. The module body stays in joyful-creation register throughout — no grounded-competence phrasing, no unease. Bridge teases M2's plan-mode discipline without delivering it. Contract honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive — unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M1 mood is joyful creation, not unease. Rule 2 is not triggered.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea names positive disciplines (orient, fix, compound, close). Key Concepts name disciplines: 'Plan mode is overhead on a trivial bug', 'A CLAUDE.local.md born from how you actually worked reads different from one written from a blank page.' No failure-word titles.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — draft doesn't front-run a downstream teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No Skill use at M1 (per strategy doc: 'No Skill use at M1. First Skill use, first subagent, first authoring all land together at M3.'). Module correctly uses /context (slash command) and MCP (connector), not Skill invocation. Three-block memory seeded without naming per strategy. Plan mode restrained to Bridge/tease. All downstream moments intact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts — each bullet surfaces a theme instance.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts bullets surface: compounding (Plan mode overhead / compound step discipline), self-awareness (/context window reality + 10% misrepresentation), capture-your-world (CLAUDE.local.md born from how you actually worked), non-agentic-default (Personal vs team rules split). Two or three themes surfaced per rule 5.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs on 1-3 bullets, not a standalone themes section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts bullets are in tactical module voice. No standalone '**Themes this module instances:**' section present. Rule 6 only bans the standalone themes section, which is absent.
